One Song
Architecture

Play exactly one song.

A minimal Android music player that does one thing perfectly. No playlists, no libraries. Just your song, always ready.

Open source · MIT

Instant play

Open the app, your song starts. No extra taps or navigation required.

Loops forever

Repeats gaplessly until you stop it. Built natively for uninterrupted playback.

Sleep timer

Set it from 5 to 60 minutes. Choose your perfect default in settings.

Background audio

Keep the music going while the screen is locked or you're using other apps.

Lock screen controls

Play, pause, or stop directly from your lock screen or notification shade.

Remembers your song

Safe and persistent. The app caches your file so it never gets lost.

Perfect for

🌙

Bedtime lullabies

The exact same song every night. Let the sleep timer handle the rest.

🌧️

White noise

Loop rain sounds or brown noise continuously throughout the entire night.

🎧

Favorite songs

That one track you just can't get enough of, ready at a moment's notice.

Zero friction.

No ads. No subscriptions. No accounts. No playlists. No libraries.

Just one song, beautifully presented and always ready.

Beautifully simple.

Why I built this

Everyday, before going to bed, my kid wants to hear the exact same lullaby. We used to play it on YouTube or a standard music app, which meant navigating through UI clutter, playlists, and multiple taps.

I wanted a dedicated app precisely for this scenario. Instead of clicking through menus, I want the app to start playing as soon as I open it. And after it reaches the sleep timer, or I explicitly stop it, the app should quietly close.

Ashik Nesin Dad & Developer
